briefing.opening: Good morning/afternoon, everyone. I will now begin the climate briefing for May 26, 2026.

sections.title: 🔴 Intensification of the Climate Crisis and Widespread Impacts
sections.content: Recently, climate change has been sounding serious alarms for ecosystems and human health, both domestically and internationally. Domestically, the habitats of venomous snakes are being reshaped [1, 4], and in the alpine ecosystem of Jirisan, mass die-offs of Korean fir trees have been observed [5]. Internationally, heatwaves linked to climate change are increasing the risk of preterm birth in 13 countries [41], and research indicates that they are accelerating global antibiotic resistance by promoting the growth of antibiotic-resistant Salmonella [35, 36, 58]. Furthermore, record-breaking heatwaves across Western Europe have led to 7 deaths in France and new May temperature records in several countries [57, 59], demonstrating the clear footprints of climate change everywhere [51, 52, 61].

sections.title: 🇰🇷 Domestic Climate Response Efforts and Carbon Neutrality Goals
sections.content: Domestic efforts to address the climate crisis are also actively underway. In the agricultural sector, the necessity for climate change-adapted farming has been raised [3], and the Sangju Agricultural Technology Center is operating rice monitoring fields to strengthen pest and disease surveillance in response to climate change [6, 9, 10]. Ahead of local elections, arguments have been made that carbon neutrality should be chosen as a future strategy for regions [12, 17], and Jeju Province is opening a 'blue economy' by utilizing 'blue carbon' [13]. Nonsan City is providing local currency to citizens practicing carbon neutrality [14, 21], and Seongnam City is promoting the 'Sseudam Sseudam Seongnam' campaign with senior citizen clubs [18], demonstrating efforts by various local governments to spread a culture of carbon neutrality [19]. The designation of Sagimak Campground in Bukhansan National Park as the nation's first carbon-neutral campground is also a notable achievement [16].

sections.title: ⚠️ Challenges and Policy Tasks of Energy Transition
sections.content: The transition to renewable energy is showing significant progress, but simultaneously faces several challenges and tasks. While Bonghwa County and Jeonju City are achieving high performance in renewable energy dissemination and emerging as energy-independent cities [26, 27, 33], it has been pointed out that deep consideration is needed on where and how to deploy renewable energy to achieve the 100GW target by 2030 [24, 25]. Specifically, a 'bottleneck' is occurring in achieving carbon neutrality goals because the government provides subsidies for diesel vehicles but not for electric trucks [15], and arguments have been made that KEPCO (Korea Electric Power Corporation) is failing to lead innovation and that opening the electricity retail market is necessary for expanding renewable energy [23]. Furthermore, a phenomenon has been observed where the need for Liquefied Natural Gas (LNG) increases alongside the increase in solar power generation [32], indicating that more thought is needed on establishing a stable energy mix.

sections.title: 🌍 Global Society's Climate Change Reality and Search for Responses
sections.content: The international community is confronting the reality of climate change and exploring various ways to respond. Ho Chi Minh City is seeking ways to adapt to climate change and cope with floods [11], and real estate insurers are also exploring methods to respond to climate change risks [44]. According to research, climate change has emerged as the most significant factor shaping EU agricultural adaptation [56], and concerns have been raised that while rice feeds billions, its role in exacerbating climate change is growing [42]. Meanwhile, IUCN contributed global guidelines on species protection and climate action at the Antarctic Treaty Meeting [45], and in Kelowna, Canada, students are engaging in activities to address climate change [54], demonstrating ongoing international movements for climate action. However, corporate net-zero targets are being evaluated as neither greenwashing nor game-changers [49], and U.S. immigration enforcement flights are generating hundreds of thousands of tons of carbon emissions [60], suggesting that many challenges still remain.

briefing.closing: As you have seen in today's briefing, climate change is having severe impacts across various sectors globally, and multifaceted efforts to address it are urgently needed. This concludes today's climate briefing.

articles.original_title: Climate crisis is accelerating antibiotic resistance across world, study says
articles.description: Experts say climate change linked to 10% rise in salmonella antibiotic resistance genes between 1940 and 2023The climate crisis is accelerating a global increase in antibiotic resistance that poses a serious threat to human health, experts have said as figures show a rise in salmonella antibiotic resistant genes.Antibiotic resistance is one of the fastest-growing threats to global health. It can affect people of any age in any country and already kills more than 1 million people a year, accordin

summary.phenomenon: The climate crisis is accelerating antibiotic resistance globally, with a 10% increase in Salmonella antibiotic resistance genes between 1940 and 2023. Antibiotic resistance is already a serious threat, causing over 1 million deaths annually.
summary.cause: Climate change is linked to the increase in antibiotic resistance.
summary.outlook: The climate crisis is expected to globally accelerate the increase in antibiotic resistance, posing a severe threat to human health.

articles.original_title: Seven heat-related deaths in France as May records set in several countries
articles.description: Extreme early summer event across western Europe also brings highest temperatures for month in UK and IrelandSeven people have died in France in an extreme early summer heat event affecting a swathe of western Europe, with record high temperatures for May recorded for a second day in several countries.In France, which logged its hottest ever May day on Monday and again on Tuesday, the weather agency Météo France said the heatwave could last through the week and predicted temperatures could reach

summary.phenomenon: An extreme early summer heatwave across Western Europe has resulted in seven deaths in France, with several countries breaking May's highest temperature records. France recorded its hottest May days ever on Monday and Tuesday.
summary.outlook: Météo France, the French weather agency, predicted that the heatwave could persist throughout the week, with temperatures potentially rising further.

articles.title: Revealed: huge climate cost of harmful emissions from US immigration flights
articles.description: Trump campaign accelerating climate crisis as officials move migrants to detention jails and deport them from USUS immigration enforcement flights are producing hundreds of thousands of metric tonnes of climate-damaging carbon emissions as officials shuttle unprecedented numbers of people to detention centers far from home and deport them to countries across the world.Donald Trump’s mass deportation campaign has spurred at least an 80% increase in such flights year over year, accelerating the cl

summary.phenomenon: U.S. immigration enforcement flights are generating hundreds of thousands of metric tons of climate-damaging carbon emissions. Donald Trump's mass deportation campaign has led to at least an 80% year-over-year increase in such flights.
summary.cause: Carbon emissions are generated as U.S. immigration enforcement flights transport individuals to detention facilities and deport them to other countries.
summary.outlook: This increase in flights is accelerating the climate crisis.
